title: Checkpoints, Write-Ahead State, Crash Consistency, and Resume · Transfer Build
summary: An independent municipal service owner supplies a different origin, markup family, dynamic boundary, accessibility need, resource budget, and injected failure and transfers checkpoints, write-ahead state, crash consistency, and resume into a changed versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned crash-consistent crawl checkpoint with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed.
Write bounded versioned state to a temporary file, sync by declared durability, validate, rename atomically, and retain recovery copies.
Return claimed or fetching items to an explicit retry state using attempt and idempotency rules after a crash.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Checkpoint Boundary
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 4576 during an authorized municipal service audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove state boundaries, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, policy-version admission, and resume reconciliation; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Checkpoint Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define which policy, seed, frontier, attempt, digest, report, and output states must survive and which can be recomputed. For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Checkpoint Boundary, keep Checkpoint Boundary connected to executable TypeScript authorization, URL, runtime admission, Fetch, Promise, browser, accessibility, report, package, and recovery decision evidence.
